content.summary: Most commonly prescribed mud application in BNYS. Indication: constipation, IBS, indigestion, hyperacidity. Apply cool mud pack from navel to pubic symphysis for 20–30 minutes, morning before breakfast or 3 hours after meals. Cooling effect reduces intestinal inflammation and improves peristalsis.

Simple clinical marker of respiratory and cardiovascular health. Normal: 25–40 seconds. <20 sec = significant dysfunction (poor parasympathetic tone). >40 sec = good cardiovascular fitness. Measure: normal exhale, hold to first urge. Improves significantly with pranayama — useful treatment outcome marker.

30 minutes = 2–3 hours of deep sleep physiologically. Induces theta brainwave state (4–8 Hz). Evidence: ↓ cortisol, ↓ sympathetic tone, ↑ DHEA, ↓ PTSD symptoms, ↓ chronic pain scores. Prescription: 30 min daily, same time, supine. No contraindications except severe psychosis.

B — Background: 'What is going on in your life?' A — Affect: 'How do you feel?' T — Trouble: 'What troubles you most?' H — Handling: 'How are you handling it?' E — Empathy: 'That must be very difficult.' Proven to improve patient satisfaction and clinical outcomes in under 5 minutes.

Doctors interrupt patients on average within 18 seconds. If allowed to speak uninterrupted, 80% finish in under 90 seconds — and reveal the real reason for the consultation. The first 90 seconds should be the patient's time. Put down your pen. Look up. Just listen.

Correct order: Asanas → Pranayama → Meditation/Yoga Nidra → Shavasana. Never reverse. Asanas prepare the body for pranayama; pranayama prepares the mind for meditation. Pranayama on an unprepared body can cause hyperventilation and dizziness.

Alkaline-forming (eat freely): all fruits (even citrus — alkaline ASH), all vegetables, sprouts, millet, seeds, nuts, coconut water. Acid-forming (limit): meat, fish, eggs, dairy, refined grains, sugar, alcohol, coffee. It is the ASH residue after metabolism — not the taste — that determines the effect.

Expect on days 1–2: headache, fatigue, irritability, bad breath, coated tongue, body odour — signs of active detoxification, not side effects. Dangerous symptoms: severe hypoglycaemia, cardiac palpitations, BP drop >20 mmHg. Never fast patients with T1DM, active TB, Hb <8, eating disorders.

The therapeutic sensation at a correctly stimulated acupoint: dull ache, heaviness, tingling, warmth, or electric-like sensation radiating along the meridian. Absence of Deqi = incorrect location, insufficient pressure, or depleted Qi. Teach patients to recognise Deqi for self-acupressure.

Daily self-abhyanga is one of the most powerful preventive practices. Warm sesame oil, 10–15 min before morning shower. Start at crown, work down. Let oil absorb 5 min, then shower with warm water. Reduces Vata, improves sleep, ↓ cortisol, ↑ skin barrier function.

'If it isn't written, it didn't happen.' Good documentation protects the patient (continuity of care), protects you (medico-legal), and improves outcomes. Write legibly in black ink. Date and sign every entry. Never use correction fluid. BNYS case notes: keep minimum 7 years.

Always. The end-of-bed assessment gives you the gestalt of how sick the patient is. A patient who looks well despite abnormal observations is different from one who looks unwell with normal observations. Clinical intuition begins with systematic inspection.

cards.title: JVP — Window to the Right Atrium

content.summary: A raised JVP is one of the earliest signs of right heart failure. In cardiac tamponade: Beck's triad — raised JVP + hypotension + muffled heart sounds. Pulsus paradoxus also present. Always check JVP with patient at 45° and head turned slightly away.

Sesame oil: warming, anti-Vata, best for dry skin and joints. Coconut oil: cooling, anti-inflammatory, best for Pitta and skin. Mustard oil: deeply warming — cold/flu, arthritis. Neem oil: antimicrobial, antifungal — skin infections. Castor oil: anti-inflammatory — abdominal application, joint swelling.

Best therapeutic mud: black cotton soil or fullers earth (multani mitti) — high mineral content, fine texture. Collect from 3–4 feet depth (free of contaminants). Sun-dry, powder, sieve, store in earthen pot. Never use mud from near chemical factories or stagnant water sources.
